methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Precision AI is a Series A-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector founded by Sreeram Raghavan and Matt Borzilleri. The company has raised $20M to date. The comparable universe consists of eight private companies operating in Drones & Autonomous, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Rainmaker. No public comps exist in this narrow segment.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and provides the primary valuation anchor. Using the $20M raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $40M. The Peer-Median method is not applicable; while four same-sector peers exist, insufficient narrow-filtered datapoints (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier) meet the minimum threshold of three comparable companies needed for reliable triangulation. A broader sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of drone and autonomous companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $34M to $46M, with a median of $40M. This range reflects standard Series A step-up logic applied to total capital deployed. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue, unit economics, and technology differentiation—factors not captured in stage-based progression alone. A peer-based cross-check remains infeasible until additional same-stage or comparable-tier companies can be identified. Investor conviction should be grounded primarily in operational and technical diligence rather than market comparables.
